title: Towards verifying unsafe Rust programs against Rust's pointer-aliasing restrictions
authors: Wannes Tas, Bart Jacobs
abstract: The Rust programming language is famous for its strong ownership regime: at each point, each value is either exclusively owned, exclusively borrowed through a mutable reference, or borrowed as read-only through one or more shared references. These rules, known as Rust's pointer-aliasing rules, are exploited by the Rust compiler to generate more efficient machine code, and enforced by Rust's static type system, except inside unsafe blocks. In this paper, we present our work in progress towards the first program logic for modularly verifying that Rust programs that use unsafe blocks comply with the pointer-aliasing rules.
title_zh: 用于验证不安全的 Rust 程序是否符合 Rust 的指针别名限制
abstract_zh: Rust编程语言以其严格的所有权机制而闻名：在任意时刻，每个值要么被独占拥有，要么通过可变引用被独占借用，要么通过一个或多个共享引用被只读借用。这些规则被称为Rust的指针别名规则，Rust编译器利用这些规则生成更高效的机器码，并由Rust的静态类型系统进行强制执行，除非在unsafe代码块中。本文我们介绍了正在进行的工作，旨在构建首个程序逻辑，用于模块化地验证使用unsafe块的Rust程序是否遵守指针别名规则。

title: Safeguarding LLMs Against Misuse and AI-Driven Malware Using Steganographic Canaries
authors: Md Raz, Venkata Sai Charan Putrevu, Meet Udeshi, Prashanth Krishnamurthy, Farshad Khorrami, Ramesh Karri
abstract: AI-powered malware increasingly exploits cloud-hosted generative-AI services and large language models (LLMs) as analysis engines for reconnaissance and code generation. Simultaneously, enterprise uploads expose sensitive documents to third-party AI vendors. Both threats converge at the AI service ingestion boundary, yet existing defenses focus on endpoints and network perimeters, leaving organizations with limited visibility once plaintext reaches an LLM service. To address this, we present a framework based on steganographic canary files: realistic documents carrying cryptographically derived identifiers embedded via complementary encoding channels. A pre-ingestion filter extracts and verifies these identifiers before LLM processing, enabling passive, format-agnostic detection without semantic classification. We support two modes of operation where Mode A marks existing sensitive documents with layered symbolic encodings (whitespace substitution, zero-width character insertion, homoglyph substitution), while Mode B generates synthetic canary documents using linguistic steganography (arithmetic coding over GPT-2), augmented with compatible symbolic layers. We model increasing document pre-processing and adversarial capability for both modes via a four-tier transport-transform taxonomy: All methods achieve 100% identifier recovery under benign and sanitization workflows (Tiers 1-2). The hybrid Mode B maintains 97% through targeted adversarial transforms (Tier 3). An end-to-end case study against an LLM-orchestrated ransomware pipeline confirms that both modes detect and block canary-bearing uploads before file encryption begins. To our knowledge, this is the first framework to systematically combine symbolic and linguistic text steganography into layered canary documents for detecting unauthorized LLM processing, evaluated against a transport-threat taxonomy tailored to AI malware.
title_zh: 使用隐写术信标防范大语言模型滥用及人工智能驱动的恶意软件
abstract_zh:
基于隐写术的“蜜罐文件”框架：用于检测未经授权的大型语言模型处理的新型防御机制

日益增长的AI驱动型恶意软件正越来越多地利用云托管的生成式AI服务和大型语言模型（LLMs）作为侦察与代码生成的分析引擎。与此同时，企业上传敏感文档至第三方AI供应商的行为也带来了数据泄露风险。这两类威胁在AI服务的输入边界处交汇，然而现有防护措施主要聚焦于终端设备和网络边界，导致一旦明文数据进入LLM服务，组织便缺乏有效可见性。为应对这一挑战，我们提出一种基于隐写术的蜜罐文件框架：通过互补编码通道，将加密派生的标识符嵌入到外观真实的文档中，形成具有隐蔽性的“蜜罐文件”。该框架在文件进入LLM处理前，通过预摄入过滤器提取并验证这些标识符，实现无需语义分类、格式无关的被动检测。

本框架支持两种运行模式：  
- **模式A**：对已有敏感文档进行多层符号隐写标记，采用空白字符替换、零宽字符插入及同形字替换等技术；  
- **模式B**：利用语言学隐写术（基于GPT-2的算术编码）生成合成蜜罐文档，并叠加兼容的符号层。

我们构建了一个四层“传输-转换”威胁分类体系，以建模不同场景下的文档预处理复杂度与对抗能力。实验结果表明，在良性环境及清洗流程下（第1-2级），所有方法均实现100%的标识符恢复率。在针对特定对抗性变换的测试中（第3级），混合模式B仍保持97%的恢复率。一项端到端案例研究针对由LLM驱动的勒索软件攻击链进行验证，结果表明，两种模式均可在文件被加密之前成功检测并阻断携带蜜罐标识的上传行为。

据我们所知，这是首个系统性地将符号隐写与语言学隐写相结合，构建多层次蜜罐文档以检测未经授权LLM处理的框架，并首次针对AI恶意软件特性设计了专门的运输威胁分类体系，具备重要的理论价值与实践意义。

title: CirrusBench: Evaluating LLM-based Agents Beyond Correctness in Real-World Cloud Service Environments
authors: Yi Yu, Guangquan Hu, Chenghuang Shen, Xingyan Liu, Jing Gu, Hangyi Sun, Junzhuo Ma, Weiting Liu, Jianfeng Liu, Mingyue Pu, Yu Wang, Zhengdong Xiao, Rui Xie, Longjiu Luo, Qianrong Wang, Gurong Cui, Honglin Qiao, Wenlian Lu
abstract: The increasing agentic capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) have enabled their deployment in real-world applications, such as cloud services, where customer-assistant interactions exhibit high technical complexity and long-horizon dependencies, making robustness and resolution efficiency critical for customer satisfaction. However, existing benchmarks for LLM-based agents largely rely on synthetic environments that fail to capture the diversity and unpredictability of authentic customer inputs, often ignoring the resolution efficiency essential for real-world deployment. To bridge this gap, we introduce CirrusBench, a novel evaluation framework distinguished by its foundation in real-world data from authentic cloud service tickets. CirrusBench preserves the intricate multi-turn logical chains and realistic tool dependencies inherent to technical service environments. Moving beyond execution correctness, we introduce novel Customer-Centric metrics to define agent success, quantifying service quality through metrics such as the Normalized Efficiency Index and Multi-Turn Latency to explicitly measure resolution efficiency. Experiments utilizing our framework reveal that while state-of-the-art models demonstrate strong reasoning capabilities, they frequently struggle in complex, realistic multi-turn tasks and fail to meet the high-efficiency standards required for customer service, highlighting critical directions for the future development of LLM-based agents in practical technical service applications. CirrusBench evaluation framework is released at: https://github.com/CirrusAI
title_zh: CirrusBench：在真实云服务环境中超越正确性评估基于大模型的智能体
abstract_zh:
大型语言模型（LLMs）日益增强的自主能力使其得以在真实世界的应用中部署，例如云服务领域。在这些场景中，客户与助手之间的交互具有高度的技术复杂性以及长周期依赖关系，因此模型的鲁棒性和解决效率对客户满意度至关重要。然而，现有的基于LLM的智能体评估基准大多依赖于合成环境，难以捕捉真实客户输入的多样性与不可预测性，且常常忽视了实际部署中至关重要的解决效率。

为弥合这一差距，我们提出了CirrusBench——一个基于真实云服务工单数据的新型评估框架。该框架以真实世界数据为基础，完整保留了技术客服环境中固有的复杂多轮逻辑链条和真实的工具依赖关系。超越传统的执行正确性评价，我们引入了全新的“以客户为中心”的评估指标，通过诸如归一化效率指数（Normalized Efficiency Index）和多轮延迟（Multi-Turn Latency）等量化指标，明确衡量问题解决的效率。

利用该框架开展的实验表明，尽管当前最先进的模型展现出强大的推理能力，但在复杂的、贴近现实的多轮任务中仍频繁表现不佳，无法满足客户服务所需的高效标准。这揭示了未来LLM驱动的智能体在实际技术客服应用中亟需改进的关键方向。

CirrusBench评估框架已开源，地址为：https://github.com/CirrusAI

title: Evaluating Privilege Usage of Agents on Real-World Tools
authors: Quan Zhang, Lianhang Fu, Lvsi Lian, Gwihwan Go, Yujue Wang, Chijin Zhou, Yu Jiang, Geguang Pu
abstract: Equipping LLM agents with real-world tools can substantially improve productivity. However, granting agents autonomy over tool use also transfers the associated privileges to both the agent and the underlying LLM. Improper privilege usage may lead to serious consequences, including information leakage and infrastructure damage. While several benchmarks have been built to study agents' security, they often rely on pre-coded tools and restricted interaction patterns. Such crafted environments differ substantially from the real-world, making it hard to assess agents' security capabilities in critical privilege control and usage. Therefore, we propose GrantBox, a security evaluation sandbox for analyzing agent privilege usage. GrantBox automatically integrates real-world tools and allows LLM agents to invoke genuine privileges, enabling the evaluation of privilege usage under prompt injection attacks. Our results indicate that while LLMs exhibit basic security awareness and can block some direct attacks, they remain vulnerable to more sophisticated attacks, resulting in an average attack success rate of 84.80% in carefully crafted scenarios.
title_zh: 评估代理在真实世界工具中对权限的使用情况
abstract_zh: 赋予大型语言模型（LLM）代理真实世界工具可显著提升其生产力。然而，赋予代理对工具使用的自主权，也意味着将相关权限同时授予了代理及其底层的LLM。不当的权限使用可能导致严重后果，包括信息泄露和基础设施损坏。尽管已有若干基准测试被用于研究代理的安全性，但这些基准通常依赖于预编码的工具和受限的交互模式。这类人为构建的环境与真实世界存在显著差异，难以有效评估代理在关键权限控制与使用方面的安全能力。因此，我们提出了GrantBox——一个用于分析代理权限使用情况的安全评估沙箱。GrantBox能够自动集成真实世界工具，并允许LLM代理调用真实的系统权限，从而在提示注入攻击等场景下评估权限使用的安全性。我们的实验结果表明，尽管LLM具备一定的安全意识并能阻止部分直接攻击，但仍容易受到更复杂的攻击，导致在精心设计的攻击场景中平均攻击成功率高达84.80%。

title: CoT2-Meta: Budgeted Metacognitive Control for Test-Time Reasoning
authors: Siyuan Ma, Bo Gao, Zikai Xiao, Hailong Wang, Xinlei Yu, Rui Qian, Jiayu Qian, Luqi Gong, Yang Liu
abstract: Recent test-time reasoning methods improve performance by generating more candidate chains or searching over larger reasoning trees, but they typically lack explicit control over when to expand, what to prune, how to repair, and when to abstain. We introduce CoT2-Meta, a training-free metacognitive reasoning framework that combines object-level chain-of-thought generation with meta-level control over partial reasoning trajectories. The framework integrates four components: strategy-conditioned thought generation, tree-structured search, an online process oracle for step-level reasoning evaluation, and a meta-controller that allocates computation through expansion, pruning, repair, stopping, and fallback decisions. Under matched inference budgets, CoT2-Meta consistently outperforms strong single-path, sampling-based, and search-based baselines, including ReST-MCTS. On the default backbone, it achieves 92.8 EM on MATH, 90.4 accuracy on GPQA, 98.65 EM on GSM8K, 75.8 accuracy on BBEH, 85.6 accuracy on MMMU-Pro, and 48.8 accuracy on HLE, with gains over the strongest non-CoT2-Meta baseline of +3.6, +5.2, +1.15, +2.0, +4.3, and +4.3 points, respectively. Beyond these core results, the framework remains effective across a broader 15-benchmark suite spanning knowledge and QA, multi-hop reasoning, coding, and out-of-distribution evaluation. Additional analyses show better compute scaling, improved calibration, stronger selective prediction, targeted repair behavior, and consistent gains across backbone families. These results suggest that explicit metacognitive control is a practical design principle for reliable and compute-efficient test-time reasoning systems.
title_zh: CoT2-Meta：测试时推理的预算型元认知控制
abstract_zh: 近期的测试时推理方法通过生成更多候选推理路径或在更大的推理树中进行搜索，提升了性能，但通常缺乏对何时扩展、如何剪枝、怎样修复以及何时放弃等关键决策的显式控制。我们提出了 CoT2-Meta——一种无需训练的元认知推理框架，该框架将对象级思维链生成与对部分推理轨迹的元级控制相结合。该框架包含四个核心组件：策略条件化的思维生成、树状结构搜索、用于步骤级推理评估的在线过程判别器，以及一个通过扩展、剪枝、修复、停止和回退等决策分配计算资源的元控制器。在相同的推理预算下，CoT2-Meta 始终优于多种强大的单路径、采样型及搜索型基线方法，包括 ReST-MCTS。在默认主干模型上，其在 MATH 上达到 92.8 的 EM 分数，在 GPQA 上取得 90.4 的准确率，在 GSM8K 上达到 98.65 的 EM 分数，在 BBEH 上为 75.8 的准确率，在 MMMU-Pro 上为 85.6 的准确率，在 HLE 上为 48.8 的准确率，相较于最强的非 CoT2-Meta 基线分别提升了 +3.6、+5.2、+1.15、+2.0、+4.3 和 +4.3 个百分点。除了上述核心结果外，该框架在涵盖知识与问答、多跳推理、编程任务以及分布外评估的更广泛 15 个基准测试中也表现出色。额外分析表明，该框架具备更优的计算资源扩展性、更好的校准能力、更强的选择性预测能力、有针对性的修复行为，且在不同主干模型家族中均能保持稳定提升。这些结果表明，显式的元认知控制是一种切实可行的设计原则，有助于构建可靠且计算高效的测试时推理系统。

title: DAInfer+: Neurosymbolic Inference of API Specifications from Documentation via Embedding Models
authors: Maryam Masoudian, Anshunkang Zhou, Chengpeng Wang, Charles Zhang
abstract: Modern software systems heavily rely on various libraries, which require understanding the API semantics in static analysis. However, summarizing API semantics remains challenging due to complex implementations or unavailable library code. This paper presents DAInfer+, a novel approach for inferring API specifications from library documentation. We employ Natural Language Processing (NLP) to interpret informal semantic information provided by the documentation, which enables us to reduce the specification inference to an optimization problem. Specifically, we investigate the effectiveness of sentence embedding models and Large Language Models (LLMs) in deriving memory operation abstractions from API descriptions. These abstractions are used to retrieve data-flow and aliasing relations to generate comprehensive API specifications. To solve the optimization problem efficiently, we propose neurosymbolic optimization, yielding precise data-flow and aliasing specifications. Our evaluation of popular Java libraries shows that zero-shot sentence embedding models outperform few-shot prompted LLMs in robustness, capturing fine-grained semantic nuances more effectively. While our initial attempts using two-stage LLM prompting yielded promising results, we found that the embedding-based approach proved superior. Specifically, these models achieve over 82% recall and 85% precision for data-flow inference and 88% recall and 79% precision for alias relations, all within seconds. These results demonstrate the practical value of DAInfer+ in library-aware static analysis.
title_zh: DAInfer+：基于嵌入模型的神经符号推理，从文档中推断API规范
abstract_zh: 现代软件系统高度依赖各种库，而静态分析需要理解API的语义。然而，由于实现复杂或库代码不可用，总结API语义仍然具有挑战性。本文提出了一种名为DAInfer+的新方法，用于从库文档中推断API规范。我们采用自然语言处理（NLP）技术解析文档提供的非正式语义信息，从而将规范推断问题转化为一个优化问题。具体而言，我们研究了句子嵌入模型和大语言模型（LLMs）在从API描述中提取内存操作抽象方面的有效性。这些抽象被用来检索数据流和别名关系，进而生成全面的API规范。为高效求解该优化问题，我们提出了神经符号优化方法，能够生成精确的数据流与别名规范。对主流Java库的评估表明，零样本句子嵌入模型在鲁棒性方面优于少样本提示的LLM，更有效地捕捉细微的语义差异。尽管我们最初尝试使用两阶段LLM提示法取得了有希望的结果，但最终发现基于嵌入的方法表现更优。具体而言，这些模型在数据流推断上实现了超过82%的召回率和85%的精确率，在别名关系推断上达到88%的召回率和79%的精确率，所有计算均在数秒内完成。这些结果充分展示了DAInfer+在面向库的静态分析中的实际价值。

title: COvolve: Adversarial Co-Evolution of Large-Language-Model-Generated Policies and Environments via Two-Player Zero-Sum Game
authors: Alkis Sygkounas, Rishi Hazra, Andreas Persson, Pedro Zuidberg Dos Martires, Amy Loutfi
abstract: A central challenge in building continually improving agents is that training environments are typically static or manually constructed. This restricts continual learning and generalization beyond the training distribution. We address this with COvolve, a co-evolutionary framework that leverages large language models (LLMs) to generate both environments and agent policies, expressed as executable Python code. We model the interaction between environment and policy designers as a two-player zero-sum game, ensuring adversarial co-evolution in which environments expose policy weaknesses and policies adapt in response. This process induces an automated curriculum in which environments and policies co-evolve toward increasing complexity. To guarantee robustness and prevent forgetting as the curriculum progresses, we compute the mixed-strategy Nash equilibrium (MSNE) of the zero-sum game, thereby yielding a meta-policy. This MSNE meta-policy ensures that the agent does not forget to solve previously seen environments while learning to solve previously unseen ones. Experiments in urban driving, symbolic maze-solving, and geometric navigation showcase that COvolve produces progressively more complex environments. Our results demonstrate the potential of LLM-driven co-evolution to achieve open-ended learning without predefined task distributions or manual intervention.
title_zh: COvolve：通过二人零和博弈实现大语言模型生成策略与环境的对抗性共同进化
abstract_zh:
构建持续改进智能体的一个核心挑战在于，训练环境通常是静态的或人为构造的，这限制了持续学习能力以及对训练分布之外情况的泛化能力。为解决这一问题，我们提出了COvolve——一种协同进化框架，利用大语言模型（LLMs）自动生成环境和智能体策略，并以可执行的Python代码形式表达。我们将环境设计者与策略设计者之间的互动建模为一个双人零和博弈，从而实现对抗性的协同进化：环境暴露策略的弱点，而策略则相应地进行适应性调整。这一过程自动催生出一个不断演进的学习课程，使环境与策略共同向更高复杂度发展。

为了确保在课程推进过程中系统的鲁棒性并防止遗忘，我们计算该零和博弈的混合策略纳什均衡（MSNE），由此获得一种元策略。该MSNE元策略能够保证智能体在学习解决新出现环境的同时，不会遗忘已见过环境的求解能力。在城市驾驶、符号迷宫求解和几何导航等任务上的实验表明，COvolve能够生成越来越复杂的环境。结果证明，基于大语言模型的协同进化方法具有实现无边界学习的巨大潜力，无需预设任务分布，也无需人工干预。

title: Warp-STAR: High-performance, Differentiable GPU-Accelerated Static Timing Analysis through Warp-oriented Parallel Orchestration
authors: En-Ming Huang, Shih-Hao Hung
abstract: Static timing analysis (STA) is crucial for Electronic Design Automation (EDA) flows but remains a computational bottleneck. While existing GPU-based STA engines are faster than CPU, they suffer from inefficiencies, particularly intra-warp load imbalance caused by irregular circuit graphs. This paper introduces Warp-STAR, a novel GPU-accelerated STA engine that eliminates this imbalance by orchestrating parallel computations at the warp level. This approach achieves a 2.4X speedup over previous state-of-the-art (SoTA) GPU-based STA. When integrated into a timing-driven global placement framework, Warp-STAR delivers a 1.7X speedup over SoTA frameworks. The method also proves effective for differentiable gradient analysis with minimal overhead.
title_zh: Warp-STAR：通过瓦片导向的并行调度实现高性能、可微分的GPU加速静态时序分析
abstract_zh: 静态时序分析（STA）在电子设计自动化（EDA）流程中至关重要，但仍是计算上的瓶颈。尽管现有的基于GPU的STA引擎比CPU更快，但仍存在效率问题，尤其是由于电路图的不规则性导致的线程束内负载不平衡。本文提出了一种新型的GPU加速STA引擎——Warp-STAR，通过在线程束级别协调并行计算，彻底消除了这种负载不平衡。该方法相比之前最先进的GPU-based STA实现了2.4倍的加速。当集成到时序驱动的全局布局框架中时，Warp-STAR相较于最先进的框架实现了1.7倍的加速。此外，该方法在可微分梯度分析中也表现出色，且开销极小。

title: Deep Research of Deep Research: From Transformer to Agent, From AI to AI for Science
authors: Yipeng Yu
abstract: With the advancement of large language models (LLMs) in their knowledge base and reasoning capabilities, their interactive modalities have evolved from pure text to multimodality and further to agentic tool use. Consequently, their applications have broadened from question answering to AI assistants and now to general-purpose agents. Deep research (DR) represents a prototypical vertical application for general-purpose agents, which represents an ideal approach for intelligent information processing and assisting humans in discovering and solving problems, with the goal of reaching or even surpassing the level of top human scientists. This paper provides a deep research of deep research. We articulate a clear and precise definition of deep research and unify perspectives from industry's deep research and academia's AI for Science (AI4S) within a developmental framework. We position LLMs and Stable Diffusion as the twin pillars of generative AI, and lay out a roadmap evolving from the Transformer to agents. We examine the progress of AI4S across various disciplines. We identify the predominant paradigms of human-AI interaction and prevailing system architectures, and discuss the major challenges and fundamental research issues that remain. AI supports scientific innovation, and science also can contribute to AI growth (Science for AI, S4AI). We hope this paper can help bridge the gap between the AI and AI4S communities.
title_zh: 深度研究的深度研究：从Transformer到智能体，从人工智能到人工智能科学
abstract_zh: 随着大型语言模型（LLMs）在知识库和推理能力方面的不断进步，其交互模态已从纯文本发展为多模态，进而演进至代理式工具使用。因此，其应用范围也从问答系统扩展到AI助手，如今正迈向通用智能体。深度研究（Deep Research, DR）作为通用智能体的典型垂直应用场景，代表了一种理想的智能化信息处理方式，旨在辅助人类发现并解决科学问题，目标是达到甚至超越顶尖人类科学家的水平。本文对“深度研究”进行了深入探讨。我们清晰而精确地定义了深度研究，并在发展框架下统一了产业界深度研究与学术界人工智能赋能科学（AI for Science, AI4S）的视角。我们将大型语言模型（LLMs）与Stable Diffusion视为生成式人工智能的两大支柱，勾勒出从Transformer到智能体的发展路线图。我们考察了AI4S在各学科领域的进展，识别出人机交互的主要范式与主流系统架构，并讨论了当前仍面临的主要挑战与基础性研究问题。人工智能支持科学创新，而科学同样能推动人工智能的发展（科学赋能AI，S4AI）。我们希望本文能够促进AI与AI4S领域之间的沟通与融合。

title: Crossing the NL/PL Divide: Information Flow Analysis Across the NL/PL Boundary in LLM-Integrated Code
authors: Zihao Xu, Xiao Cheng, Ruijie Meng, Yuekang Li
abstract: LLM API calls are becoming a ubiquitous program construct, yet they create a boundary that no existing program analysis can cross: runtime values enter a natural-language prompt, undergo opaque processing inside the LLM, and re-emerge as code, SQL, JSON, or text that the program consumes. Every analysis that tracks data across function boundaries, including taint analysis, program slicing, dependency analysis, and change-impact analysis, relies on dataflow summaries of callee behavior. LLM calls have no such summaries, breaking all of these analyses at what we call the NL/PL boundary. We present the first information flow method to bridge this boundary. Grounded in quantitative information flow theory, our taxonomy defines 24 labels along two orthogonal dimensions: information preservation level (from lexically preserved to fully blocked) and output modality (natural language, structured format, executable artifact). We label 9,083 placeholder-output pairs from 4,154 real-world Python files and validate reliability with Cohen's $κ= 0.82$ and near-complete coverage (0.01\% unclassifiable). We demonstrate the taxonomy's utility on two downstream applications: (1)~a two-stage taint propagation pipeline combining taxonomy-based filtering with LLM verification achieves $F_1 = 0.923$ on 353 expert-annotated pairs, with cross-language validation on six real-world OpenClaw prompt injection cases further confirming effectiveness; (2)~taxonomy-informed backward slicing reduces slice size by a mean of 15\% in files containing non-propagating placeholders. Per-label analysis reveals that four blocked labels account for nearly all non-propagating cases, providing actionable filtering criteria for tool builders.
title_zh: 跨越NL/PL的界限：在LLM集成代码中对NL/PL边界的信息流分析
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）API调用正逐渐成为一种普遍的程序构造，但它们在现有程序分析中制造了一个无法跨越的边界：运行时值进入自然语言提示，经过LLM内部的不透明处理后，以代码、SQL、JSON或文本等形式重新输出，供程序消费。所有跨函数边界的分析——包括污点分析、程序切片、依赖分析和变更影响分析——都依赖于对被调用函数行为的数据流摘要。然而，LLM调用缺乏此类摘要，导致这些分析在我们称之为“自然语言/编程语言”（NL/PL）边界的环节中断。本文提出了首个能够跨越该边界的信道信息流方法。基于量化信息流理论，我们的分类体系定义了24个标签，沿两个正交维度展开：信息保留程度（从词法保留到完全阻断）与输出模态（自然语言、结构化格式、可执行实体）。我们对来自4,154个真实世界Python文件的9,083个占位符-输出对进行了标注，并通过Cohen's κ = 0.82 的可靠性检验以及近乎完整的覆盖率（仅0.01%无法分类）验证了其有效性。我们在两个下游应用中展示了该分类体系的实用性：（1）一种两阶段污点传播管道，结合基于分类的过滤与LLM验证，在353个专家标注对上实现了F₁ = 0.923的性能，且在六个真实世界的OpenClaw提示注入案例中进行跨语言验证，进一步证实了其有效性；（2）基于分类信息的反向切片方法，在包含非传播性占位符的文件中，平均将切片规模减少了15%。各标签的分析表明，四个“阻断”类标签几乎涵盖了所有非传播情况，为工具开发者提供了可操作的过滤标准。

title: Kernel-Smith: A Unified Recipe for Evolutionary Kernel Optimization
authors: He Du, Qiming Ge, Jiakai Hu, Aijun Yang, Zheng Cai, Zixian Huang, Sheng Yuan, Qinxiu Cheng, Xinchen Xie, Yicheng Chen, Yining Li, Jiaxing Xie, Huanan Dong, Yaguang Wu, Xiangjun Huang, Jian Yang, Hui Wang, Bowen Zhou, Bowen Li, Qipeng Guo, Kai Chen
abstract: We present Kernel-Smith, a framework for high-performance GPU kernel and operator generation that combines a stable evaluation-driven evolutionary agent with an evolution-oriented post-training recipe. On the agent side, Kernel-Smith maintains a population of executable candidates and iteratively improves them using an archive of top-performing and diverse programs together with structured execution feedback on compilation, correctness, and speedup. To make this search reliable, we build backend-specific evaluation services for Triton on NVIDIA GPUs and Maca on MetaX GPUs. On the training side, we convert long-horizon evolution trajectories into step-centric supervision and reinforcement learning signals by retaining correctness-preserving, high-gain revisions, so that the model is optimized as a strong local improver inside the evolutionary loop rather than as a one-shot generator. Under a unified evolutionary protocol, Kernel-Smith-235B-RL achieves state-of-the-art overall performance on KernelBench with Nvidia Triton backend, attaining the best average speedup ratio and outperforming frontier proprietary models including Gemini-3.0-pro and Claude-4.6-opus. We further validate the framework on the MetaX MACA backend, where our Kernel-Smith-MACA-30B surpasses large-scale counterparts such as DeepSeek-V3.2-think and Qwen3-235B-2507-think, highlighting potential for seamless adaptation across heterogeneous platforms. Beyond benchmark results, the same workflow produces upstream contributions to production systems including SGLang and LMDeploy, demonstrating that LLM-driven kernel optimization can transfer from controlled evaluation to practical deployment.
title_zh: Kernel-Smith：一种统一的进化核优化配方
abstract_zh: 我们提出 Kernel-Smith，一个用于高性能 GPU 内核与算子生成的框架，该框架结合了稳定评估驱动的进化代理与面向进化的后训练优化方案。在代理端，Kernel-Smith 维护一组可执行候选程序，并通过一个包含顶尖且多样化程序的归档库，以及对编译、正确性与加速比的结构化执行反馈，持续迭代优化这些候选程序。为确保搜索过程的可靠性，我们为 NVIDIA GPU 上的 Triton 和 MetaX GPU 上的 Maca 分别构建了后端专用的评估服务。在训练端，我们通过保留保持正确性且收益显著的修改，将长时程的进化轨迹转化为以步骤为中心的监督信号与强化学习信号，使模型在进化循环中被优化为强大的局部改进者，而非一次性生成器。在统一的进化协议下，Kernel-Smith-235B-RL 在使用 NVIDIA Triton 后端的 KernelBench 基准测试中达到业界领先的整体性能，实现了最佳平均加速比，并超越了包括 Gemini-3.0-pro 和 Claude-4.6-opus 在内的前沿专有模型。我们进一步在 MetaX MACA 后端验证了该框架的有效性，其中 Kernel-Smith-MACA-30B 的表现优于 DeepSeek-V3.2-think 和 Qwen3-235B-2507-think 等大规模模型，凸显其在异构平台间无缝迁移的巨大潜力。除了基准测试结果外，该工作流程还成功贡献于 SGLang 和 LMDeploy 等生产系统，证明了基于大语言模型的内核优化技术能够从受控评估环境顺利迁移到实际部署场景。

title: VulnScout-C: A Lightweight Transformer for C Code Vulnerability Detection
authors: Aymen Lassoued, Nacef Mbarek, Bechir Dardouri, Bassem Ouni, Qing Li, Fakhri Karray
abstract: Vulnerability detection in C programs is a critical challenge in software security. Although large language models (LLMs) achieve strong detection performance, their multi-billion-parameter scale makes them impractical for integration into development workflows requiring low latency and continuous analysis. We introduce VULNSCOUT-C, a compact transformer architecture with 693M total parameters (353M active during inference), derived from the Qwen model family and optimized for C code vulnerability detection. Alongside the model, we present VULNSCOUT, a new 33,565-sample curated dataset generated through a controlled multi-agent pipeline with formal verification, designed to fill coverage gaps in existing benchmarks across underrepresented CWE categories. Evaluated on a standardized C vulnerability detection benchmark, VULNSCOUT-C outperforms all evaluated baselines, including state-of-the-art reasoning LLMs and commercial static analysis tools, while offering a fraction of their inference cost. These results demonstrate that task-specialized compact architectures can match or even outperform the detection capability of models orders of magnitude larger, making continuous, low-latency vulnerability analysis practical within real-world development workflows.
title_zh: VulnScout-C：一种用于C语言代码漏洞检测的轻量级Transformer模型
abstract_zh: C语言程序中的漏洞检测是软件安全领域的一项关键挑战。尽管大型语言模型（LLMs）在漏洞检测方面表现出强大的性能，但其数十亿参数的规模使其难以集成到对低延迟和持续分析有要求的开发工作流中。我们提出了VULNSCOUT-C，一种基于Qwen模型家族构建的紧凑型Transformer架构，总参数量为6.93亿（推理时激活3.53亿），专为C代码漏洞检测而优化。与此同时，我们还推出了VULNSCOUT——一个由受控多智能体流水线生成、并经过形式化验证的新数据集，包含33,565个精心筛选的样本，旨在填补现有基准在代表性不足的CWE类别中的覆盖空白。在标准化的C语言漏洞检测基准上评估显示，VULNSCOUT-C在性能上超越了所有对比基线，包括最先进的推理型大语言模型和商业静态分析工具，同时推理成本仅为它们的一小部分。这些结果表明，针对特定任务设计的紧凑型模型架构能够达到甚至超过远大于自身的模型的检测能力，使得在真实开发流程中实现持续、低延迟的漏洞分析成为可能。

title: Meta-Harness: End-to-End Optimization of Model Harnesses
authors: Yoonho Lee, Roshen Nair, Qizheng Zhang, Kangwook Lee, Omar Khattab, Chelsea Finn
abstract: The performance of large language model (LLM) systems depends not only on model weights, but also on their harness: the code that determines what information to store, retrieve, and present to the model. Yet harnesses are still designed largely by hand, and existing text optimizers are poorly matched to this setting because they compress feedback too aggressively. We introduce Meta-Harness, an outer-loop system that searches over harness code for LLM applications. It uses an agentic proposer that accesses the source code, scores, and execution traces of all prior candidates through a filesystem. On online text classification, Meta-Harness improves over a state-of-the-art context management system by 7.7 points while using 4x fewer context tokens. On retrieval-augmented math reasoning, a single discovered harness improves accuracy on 200 IMO-level problems by 4.7 points on average across five held-out models. On agentic coding, discovered harnesses surpass the best hand-engineered baselines on TerminalBench-2. Together, these results show that richer access to prior experience can enable automated harness engineering.
title_zh: Meta-Harness：模型 harness 的端到端优化
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）系统的性能不仅取决于模型权重，还依赖于其“Harness”——即决定哪些信息应被存储、检索并呈现给模型的代码。然而，目前Harness的设计仍主要依靠人工完成，而现有的文本优化工具在这一场景下表现不佳，因为它们对反馈信息的压缩过于激进。我们提出了Meta-Harness，一种用于LLM应用的外层搜索系统，能够自动探索Harness代码空间。该系统采用一个智能体式提案器（agentic proposer），通过文件系统访问所有先前候选方案的源代码、评分结果以及执行轨迹。在在线文本分类任务中，Meta-Harness相较于当前最先进的上下文管理方法，性能提升7.7分，同时仅使用四分之一的上下文token；在基于检索增强的数学推理任务中，单一发现的Harness使五种独立测试模型在200道国际数学奥林匹克（IMO）级别问题上的平均准确率提升了4.7分；在智能体编程任务中，所发现的Harness在TerminalBench-2基准上超越了最佳的手工设计基线。这些结果表明，对过往经验更丰富的访问能力，能够实现自动化Harness工程的突破。

title: Superset Decompilation
authors: Chang Liu, Yihao Sun, Thomas Gilray, Kristopher Micinski
abstract: Reverse engineering tools remain monolithic and imperative compared to the advancement of modern compiler architectures: analyses are tied to a single mutable representation, making them difficult to extend or refine, and forcing premature choices between soundness and precision. We observe that decompilation is the reverse of compilation and can be structured as a sequence of modular passes, each performing a granular and clearly defined interpretation of the binary at a progressively higher level of abstraction. We formalize this as provenance-guided superset decompilation (PGSD), a framework that monotonically derives facts about the binary into a relation store. Instead of committing early to a single interpretation, the pipeline retains ambiguous interpretations as parallel candidates with provenance, deferring resolution until the final selection phase. Manifold implements PGSD as a declarative reverse engineering framework that lifts Linux ELF binaries to C99 through a granular intermediate representation in ~35K lines of Rust and Datalog. On GNU coreutils, Manifold's output quality matches Ghidra, IDA Pro, angr, and RetDec on multiple metrics while producing fewer compiler errors, and generalizes across compilers and optimization levels.
title_zh: 超集反编译
abstract_zh: 与现代编译器架构的快速发展相比，逆向工程工具仍保持单体化和命令式的设计：分析过程依赖于单一可变的中间表示，导致其难以扩展或改进，并迫使开发者过早地在正确性与精度之间做出权衡。我们观察到，反汇编本质上是编译的逆过程，可以被组织为一系列模块化的处理阶段，每个阶段对二进制文件进行粒度精细且定义清晰的语义解释，逐步提升抽象层次。为此，我们提出了**溯源引导的超集反汇编**（Provenance-Guided Superset Decompilation, PGSD）框架，该框架以单调方式将关于二进制文件的事实推导至一个关系存储中。与早期确定唯一解释不同，该流水线保留模糊解释作为并行候选方案，并附带溯源信息，将最终解析推迟到选择阶段。Manifold 实现了 PGSD，作为一个声明式的逆向工程框架，通过约 3.5 万行 Rust 和 Datalog 代码，将 Linux ELF 二进制文件逐步提升至 C99 语言。在 GNU coreutils 上的实验表明，Manifold 的输出质量在多个指标上与 Ghidra、IDA Pro、angr 和 RetDec 相当，同时生成更少的编译错误，并能有效泛化至不同编译器及优化级别。

title: InconLens: Interactive Visual Diagnosis of Behavioral Inconsistencies in LLM-based Agentic Systems
authors: Shuo Yan, Xiaolin Wen, Shaolun Ruan, Yanjie Zhang, Jiaming Mi, Yushi Sun, Huamin Qu, Rui Sheng
abstract: Large Language Model (LLM)-based agentic systems have shown growing promise in tackling complex, multi-step tasks through autonomous planning, reasoning, and interaction with external environments. However, the stochastic nature of LLM generation introduces intrinsic behavioral inconsistency: the same agent may succeed in one execution but fail in another under identical inputs. Diagnosing such inconsistencies remains a major challenge for developers, as agent execution logs are often lengthy, unstructured, and difficult to compare across runs. Existing debugging and evaluation tools primarily focus on inspecting single executions, offering limited support for understanding how and why agent behaviors diverge across repeated runs. To address this challenge, we introduce InconLens, a visual analytics system designed to support interactive diagnosis of LLM-based agentic systems with a particular focus on cross-run behavioral analysis. InconLens introduces information nodes as an intermediate abstraction that captures canonical informational milestones shared across executions, enabling semantic alignment and inspection of agent reasoning trajectories across multiple runs. We demonstrate the effectiveness of InconLens through a detailed case study and further validate its usability and analytical value via expert interviews. Our results show that InconLens enables developers to more efficiently identify divergence points, uncover latent failure modes, and gain actionable insights into improving the reliability and stability of agentic systems.
title_zh: InconLens：面向基于大模型代理系统的行为不一致性的交互式可视化诊断
abstract_zh:
基于大语言模型（LLM）的智能体系统在通过自主规划、推理以及与外部环境交互来处理复杂、多步骤任务方面展现出日益增长的潜力。然而，LLM生成过程固有的随机性带来了内在的行为不一致性：相同的智能体在相同输入下，可能在一次执行中成功，而在另一次执行中失败。这种行为不一致性的诊断对开发者而言仍是一个重大挑战，因为智能体的执行日志通常冗长、非结构化，且跨运行之间的比较极为困难。现有的调试与评估工具主要聚焦于单次执行的检查，难以有效支持理解智能体在多次运行中行为差异的原因与机制。

为应对这一挑战，我们提出了InconLens——一个面向交互式诊断的可视化分析系统，特别关注跨运行的行为分析。InconLens引入了“信息节点”作为中间抽象，捕捉各次执行中共享的典型信息里程碑，从而实现不同运行间智能体推理轨迹的语义对齐与对比分析。通过一个详尽的案例研究，我们展示了InconLens的有效性，并进一步通过专家访谈验证了其易用性与分析价值。结果表明，InconLens能够帮助开发者更高效地识别行为分歧点，发现潜在的故障模式，并获得可操作的洞察，以提升智能体系统的可靠性与稳定性。

title: GEMS: Agent-Native Multimodal Generation with Memory and Skills
authors: Zefeng He, Siyuan Huang, Xiaoye Qu, Yafu Li, Tong Zhu, Yu Cheng, Yang Yang
abstract: Recent multimodal generation models have achieved remarkable progress on general-purpose generation tasks, yet continue to struggle with complex instructions and specialized downstream tasks. Inspired by the success of advanced agent frameworks such as Claude Code, we propose \textbf{GEMS} (Agent-Native Multimodal \textbf{GE}neration with \textbf{M}emory and \textbf{S}kills), a framework that pushes beyond the inherent limitations of foundational models on both general and downstream tasks. GEMS is built upon three core components. Agent Loop introduces a structured multi-agent framework that iteratively improves generation quality through closed-loop optimization. Agent Memory provides a persistent, trajectory-level memory that hierarchically stores both factual states and compressed experiential summaries, enabling a global view of the optimization process while reducing redundancy. Agent Skill offers an extensible collection of domain-specific expertise with on-demand loading, allowing the system to effectively handle diverse downstream applications. Across five mainstream tasks and four downstream tasks, evaluated on multiple generative backends, GEMS consistently achieves significant performance gains. Most notably, it enables the lightweight 6B model Z-Image-Turbo to surpass the state-of-the-art Nano Banana 2 on GenEval2, demonstrating the effectiveness of agent harness in extending model capabilities beyond their original limits.
title_zh: GEMS：具备记忆与技能的代理原生多模态生成
abstract_zh:
近期的多模态生成模型在通用生成任务上取得了显著进展，但在处理复杂指令和特定下游任务时仍面临挑战。受Claude Code等先进代理框架成功的启发，我们提出了**GEMS**（原生代理多模态生成系统，包含记忆与技能），该框架突破了基础模型在通用任务及下游任务中的固有局限。GEMS基于三个核心组件构建：  
**代理循环**（Agent Loop）引入了一种结构化的多代理框架，通过闭环优化机制迭代提升生成质量；  
**代理记忆**（Agent Memory）提供一种持久的、轨迹级别的记忆机制，以分层方式存储事实状态与压缩的经验摘要，既实现了对优化过程的全局视角，又有效减少了冗余信息；  
**代理技能**（Agent Skill）则是一套可扩展的领域专用知识库，支持按需加载，使系统能够高效应对多样化的下游应用需求。  

在五个主流任务和四个下游任务上的实验表明，GEMS在多个生成后端上均表现出持续显著的性能提升。尤为突出的是，它使轻量级的6B模型Z-Image-Turbo在GenEval2评测中超越了当前最先进的Nano Banana 2模型，充分证明了代理驱动机制在拓展模型能力边界方面的有效性。

title: Compressing Code Context for LLM-based Issue Resolution
authors: Haoxiang Jia, Earl T. Barr, Sergey Mechtaev
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are now capable of resolving real-world GitHub issues. However, current approaches overapproximate the code context and suffer from two compounding problems: the prohibitive cost of processing massive inputs, and low effectiveness as noise floods the context window and distracts the model from the bug-fixing signal. Existing compression techniques fail to resolve this tension: generic compressors compromise the semantic integrity of code, while code-specific tools lack awareness of code structure and task context to preserve essential patch ingredients. To address this, we propose a novel framework consisting of two components. First, Oracle-guided Code Distillation (OCD), a context distillation algorithm that combines genetic search and delta debugging to systematically reduce code contexts to their minimal sufficient subsequence - retaining only the ingredients required for a successful fix. We use this distilled data to fine-tune SWEzze, a lightweight model that learns to compress code context at inference time, filtering noise and combating distraction while preserving fix ingredients. Evaluated on SWE-bench Verified across three frontier LLMs, SWEzze maintains a stable compression rate of about 6 times across models, reduces the total token budget by 51.8%-71.3% relative to the uncompressed setting, improves issue resolution rates by 5.0%-9.2%, and delivers the best overall balance among effectiveness, compression ratio, and latency compared with state-of-the-art context compression baselines.
title_zh: 为基于大语言模型的故障解决压缩代码上下文
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）如今已具备解决真实世界GitHub问题的能力。然而，现有方法往往过度扩展代码上下文，导致两个相互加剧的问题：处理海量输入带来的高昂计算成本，以及因噪声充斥上下文窗口而降低模型对修复信号的捕捉效率。现有的压缩技术难以缓解这一矛盾：通用压缩工具会损害代码的语义完整性，而特定于代码的工具又缺乏对代码结构和任务上下文的认知，无法有效保留修复所需的关键要素。为应对这一挑战，我们提出了一种新颖的框架，包含两个核心组件。首先，提出Oracle引导的代码精炼（Oracle-guided Code Distillation, OCD），一种结合遗传搜索与差分调试的上下文精炼算法，可系统性地将代码上下文缩减至其最小必要子序列——仅保留实现成功修复所必需的成分。利用该精炼后的数据，我们对SWEzze进行微调，这是一种轻量级模型，能够在推理阶段学习高效压缩代码上下文，有效过滤噪声、减少干扰，同时完整保留修复关键信息。在SWE-bench Verified基准上对三种前沿LLM进行评估表明，SWEzze在不同模型间保持约6倍的稳定压缩率，相较于未压缩设置，总token开销降低51.8%–71.3%，问题解决率提升5.0%–9.2%，并在有效性、压缩比与延迟之间实现了优于当前最先进上下文压缩基线的最佳综合平衡。

title: BACE: LLM-based Code Generation through Bayesian Anchored Co-Evolution of Code and Test Populations
authors: Kaushitha Silva, Srinath Perera
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated impressive capabilities in code generation. While an interactive feedback loop can improve performance, writing effective tests is a non-trivial task. Early multi-agent frameworks, such as AgentCoder, automated this process but relied on generated tests as absolute ground truth. This approach is fragile: incorrect code frequently passes faulty or trivial tests, while valid solutions are often degraded to satisfy incorrect assertions. Addressing this limitation, newer methods have largely abandoned test generation in favor of planning and reasoning based on examples. We argue, however, that generated tests remain a valuable signal if we model them as noisy sensors guided by bayesian updates. To this end, we introduce BACE (Bayesian Anchored Co-Evolution), a framework that reformulates synthesis as a Bayesian co-evolutionary process where code and test populations are evolved, guided by belief distributions that are reciprocally updated based on noisy interaction evidence. By anchoring this search on minimal public examples, BACE prevents the co-evolutionary drift typical of self-validating loops. Extensive evaluations on LiveCodeBench v6 (post-March 2025) reveal that BACE achieves superior performance across both proprietary models and open-weight small language models.
title_zh: BACE：基于贝叶斯锚定的代码与测试种群协同进化实现大模型代码生成
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在代码生成方面展现了令人印象深刻的能力。尽管交互式反馈循环能够提升性能，但编写有效的测试用例却是一项非平凡的任务。早期的多智能体框架（如 AgentCoder）曾尝试自动化这一过程，但其依赖生成的测试作为绝对真实标准，这种做法存在脆弱性：错误的代码常常能通过缺陷或过于简单的测试，而正确的解决方案则可能因需满足错误断言而被破坏。为解决这一局限，新方法大多放弃了测试生成，转而基于示例进行规划与推理。然而，我们认为，若将生成的测试视为受贝叶斯更新引导的噪声传感器，它们依然具有重要价值。为此，我们提出 BACE（Bayesian Anchored Co-Evolution，贝叶斯锚定协同进化）框架，将代码合成重新定义为一种贝叶斯协同进化过程——在此过程中，代码与测试种群共同演化，并由相互更新的信念分布指导，这些信念基于来自噪声交互证据的反馈。通过以最小的公开示例作为搜索锚点，BACE 有效防止了自验证循环中常见的协同漂移问题。在 LiveCodeBench v6（2025年3月之后版本）上的大量评估表明，BACE 在专有模型和开源小规模语言模型上均表现出卓越性能。

title: Information-Theoretic Limits of Safety Verification for Self-Improving Systems
authors: Arsenios Scrivens
abstract: Can a safety gate permit unbounded beneficial self-modification while maintaining bounded cumulative risk? We formalize this question through dual conditions -- requiring sum delta_n < infinity (bounded risk) and sum TPR_n = infinity (unbounded utility) -- and establish a theory of their (in)compatibility. Classification impossibility (Theorem 1): For power-law risk schedules delta_n = O(n^{-p}) with p > 1, any classifier-based gate under overlapping safe/unsafe distributions satisfies TPR_n <= C_alpha * delta_n^beta via Holder's inequality, forcing sum TPR_n < infinity. This impossibility is exponent-optimal (Theorem 3). A second independent proof via the NP counting method (Theorem 4) yields a 13% tighter bound without Holder's inequality. Universal finite-horizon ceiling (Theorem 5): For any summable risk schedule, the exact maximum achievable classifier utility is U*(N, B) = N * TPR_NP(B/N), growing as exp(O(sqrt(log N))) -- subpolynomial. At N = 10^6 with budget B = 1.0, a classifier extracts at most U* ~ 87 versus a verifier's ~500,000. Verification escape (Theorem 2): A Lipschitz ball verifier achieves delta = 0 with TPR > 0, escaping the impossibility. Formal Lipschitz bounds for pre-LayerNorm transformers under LoRA enable LLM-scale verification. The separation is strict. We validate on GPT-2 (d_LoRA = 147,456): conditional delta = 0 with TPR = 0.352. Comprehensive empirical validation is in the companion paper [D2].
title_zh: 自增强系统安全验证的信息论极限
abstract_zh:
能否在保持累积风险有界的情况下，允许安全闸门实现无限制的有益自我修改？我们通过两个对偶条件来形式化这一问题：要求 $\sum \delta_n < \infty$（风险有界）和 $\sum \mathrm{TPR}_n = \infty$（效用无界），并建立关于二者（不）相容性的理论。  

**分类器不可行性定理（定理1）**：对于幂律风险函数 $\delta_n = O(n^{-p})$ 且 $p > 1$ 的情形，任何基于分类器的安全闸门，若其安全与非安全分布存在重叠，则根据 Hölder 不等式，必然满足 $\mathrm{TPR}_n \leq C_\alpha \cdot \delta_n^\beta$，从而强制导致 $\sum \mathrm{TPR}_n < \infty$。该不可能性结果在指数上是紧致的（定理3）。  

**第二种独立证明（NP计数法，定理4）**：不依赖 Hölder 不等式，通过 NP 计数方法得出一个更优的界限，相比原结果提升了约 13%。  

**通用有限时域上限（定理5）**：对于任意可求和的风险调度，最优分类器所能达到的精确最大效用为 $U^*(N, B) = N \cdot \mathrm{TPR}_{NP}(B/N)$，其增长速度为 $\exp(O(\sqrt{\log N}))$——即次多项式。当 $N = 10^6$ 且预算 $B = 1.0$ 时，分类器最多提取效用 $U^* \approx 87$，而验证器可达约 500,000。  

**验证逃逸现象（定理2）**：一种 Lipschitz 球形验证器可在 $\delta = 0$ 的情况下实现 $\mathrm{TPR} > 0$，从而规避上述不可能性。我们进一步给出了预 LayerNorm 变换器在 LoRA 框架下的严格 Lipschitz 边界，使得大语言模型规模的验证成为可能。这种分离是严格的。  

我们在 GPT-2 上进行了验证（$d_{\text{LoRA}} = 147,456$）：实现条件风险 $\delta = 0$ 时，$\mathrm{TPR} = 0.352$。全面的实证验证见配套论文 [D2]。

title: ERPO: Token-Level Entropy-Regulated Policy Optimization for Large Reasoning Models
authors: Song Yu, Li Li
abstract: Reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (RLVR) has significantly advanced the reasoning capabilities of large language models. However, standard Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) typically assigns a uniform, sequence-level advantage to all tokens, thereby overlooking the intrinsic information heterogeneity along reasoning chains. We show that this coarse-grained credit assignment leads to premature entropy collapse and encourages the model to generate redundant, low-quality reasoning paths. Through systematic empirical analysis, we identify Critical Decision Pivots (CDPs): transient high-entropy states where the policy's trajectory is most sensitive to perturbations. These pivots represent the "forks in the road" where effective multi-path exploration is most crucial yet often suppressed by uniform advantage signals. Building on these insights, we propose Entropy-Regulated Policy Optimization (ERPO), which transitions the optimization focus from coarse sequences to fine-grained token dynamics. ERPO introduces three synergistic components: (i) Entropy-aware Gating, which adaptively amplifies exploration at CDPs to facilitate diverse path discovery; (ii) Bucket-based Implicit Normalization, which mitigates difficulty bias by aligning token progress windows; and (iii) Result-anchored Advantage Synthesis, which re-weights token-level signals via outcome-driven anchors. Extensive experiments on competitive mathematical benchmarks (e.g., MATH, AIME) demonstrate that ERPO significantly outperforms GRPO. Notably, ERPO not only boosts reasoning accuracy but also yields significantly more concise and robust derivation paths, establishing a new efficiency-accuracy frontier for large reasoning models.
title_zh: ERPO：用于大型推理模型的基于令牌级熵调节的策略优化
abstract_zh: 基于可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）已显著提升了大语言模型的推理能力。然而，标准的组相对策略优化（GRPO）通常为所有标记分配统一的、序列级别的优势值，从而忽略了推理链中固有的信息异质性。我们发现，这种粗粒度的信用分配方式会导致熵过早坍缩，并促使模型生成冗余且低质量的推理路径。通过系统的实证分析，我们识别出关键决策转折点（CDPs）：即政策轨迹对扰动最敏感的瞬时高熵状态。这些转折点如同“道路分叉处”，正是多路径探索最为关键的时刻，却常因均匀的优势信号而被抑制。基于上述洞见，我们提出熵调控策略优化（ERPO），将优化重点从粗粒度的序列转向细粒度的标记动态。ERPO引入三个协同作用的组件：(i) 基于熵感知的门控机制，自适应地在CDPs处增强探索，以促进多样化路径的发现；(ii) 基于桶的隐式归一化方法，通过对齐标记进展窗口来缓解难度偏差；(iii) 以结果为导向的优势合成机制，通过结果驱动的锚点重新加权标记级信号。在多个具有挑战性的数学基准测试（如MATH、AIME）上的大量实验表明，ERPO显著优于GRPO。值得注意的是，ERPO不仅提升了推理准确性，还生成了更加简洁且鲁棒的推导路径，为大型推理模型树立了新的效率与准确率新边界。

title: AXON: An Automated Netlist Optimization Framework for High-Speed Adders
authors: Tiantian Yang, Xuanle Ren, Qingdian Wan, Qi Meng
abstract: Adders are fundamental building blocks in modern digital systems, and their performance, power, and area (PPA) directly impact system efficiency. Contemporary adders typically use parallel-prefix architectures with established PPA trade-offs, but these often fail to deliver globally optimal PPA for specific design goals. Prior work lacks netlist-/cell-level awareness, and general synthesis heuristics are not adder-specific, resulting in suboptimal PPA. To address this, we propose AXON, an automated netlist optimization framework for adders. It performs design space exploration from architectural to netlist level, integrating prefix topology search with standard-cell-aware mapping via a hierarchical approach to quickly converge to near-optimal PPA solutions. We also introduce a hybrid ultra-high-speed adder combining parallel-prefix and Ling architectures to shorten the critical path. Experiments on TSMC 28nm library show AXON improves delay, area-delay product, and energy-delay product by up to 10.3%, 12.6%, and 32.1% respectively, compared to commercial synthesis tools.
title_zh: AXON：一种用于高速加法器的自动化网表优化框架
abstract_zh: 加法器是现代数字系统的基本构建模块，其性能、功耗和面积（PPA）直接影响系统的整体效率。当前的加法器通常采用并行前缀架构，具有成熟的PPA权衡关系，但这些架构往往无法针对特定设计目标实现全局最优的PPA表现。以往的研究缺乏对网表级或单元级的感知能力，且通用综合启发式方法并非专为加法器优化设计，导致最终结果的PPA表现欠佳。为此，我们提出了AXON——一种面向加法器的自动化网表优化框架。该框架从架构层到网表层进行设计空间探索，通过分层方法将前缀拓扑搜索与标准单元感知映射相结合，能够快速收敛至接近最优的PPA解决方案。此外，我们还提出了一种混合型超高速加法器，融合并行前缀与Ling架构，有效缩短关键路径。在TSMC 28nm工艺库上的实验表明，相较于商用综合工具，AXON在延迟、面积-延迟乘积和能量-延迟乘积方面分别最多提升了10.3%、12.6%和32.1%。

title: RAD-AI: Rethinking Architecture Documentation for AI-Augmented Ecosystems
authors: Oliver Aleksander Larsen, Mahyar T. Moghaddam
abstract: AI-augmented ecosystems (interconnected systems where multiple AI components interact through shared data and infrastructure) are becoming the architectural norm for smart cities, autonomous fleets, and intelligent platforms. Yet the architecture documentation frameworks practitioners rely on, arc42 and the C4 model, were designed for deterministic software and cannot capture probabilistic behavior, data-dependent evolution, or dual ML/software lifecycles. This gap carries regulatory consequence: the EU AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689) mandates technical documentation through Annex IV that no existing framework provides structured support for, with enforcement for high-risk systems beginning August 2, 2026. We present RAD-AI, a backward-compatible extension framework that augments arc42 with eight AI-specific sections and C4 with three diagram extensions, complemented by a systematic EU AI Act Annex IV compliance mapping. A regulatory coverage assessment with six experienced software-architecture practitioners provides preliminary evidence that RAD-AI increases Annex IV addressability from approximately 36% to 93% (mean rating) and demonstrates substantial improvement over existing frameworks. Comparative analysis on two production AI platforms (Uber Michelangelo, Netflix Metaflow) captures eight additional AI-specific concerns missed by standard frameworks and demonstrates that documentation deficiencies are structural rather than domain-specific. An illustrative smart mobility ecosystem case study reveals ecosystem-level concerns, including cascading drift and differentiated compliance obligations, that are invisible under standard notation.
title_zh: RAD-AI：重新思考人工智能增强生态系统的建筑文档
abstract_zh:
人工智能增强型生态系统（即多个AI组件通过共享数据与基础设施相互关联的系统）正逐渐成为智慧城市、自动驾驶车队及智能平台的主流架构范式。然而，从业者目前依赖的架构文档框架——arc42和C4模型——最初是为确定性软件设计的，无法有效描述概率性行为、数据驱动的演化过程，以及机器学习与软件并行的双重生命周期。这一差距带来了显著的监管风险：欧盟《人工智能法案》（Regulation 2024/1689）在附件IV中明确规定了技术文档要求，但现有框架均未提供结构化支持，且针对高风险系统的合规 enforcement 将从2026年8月2日起正式实施。

为此，我们提出RAD-AI——一个向后兼容的扩展框架，它在arc42中新增八个面向AI的文档章节，在C4模型中引入三个图表扩展，并配套一套系统化的欧盟《人工智能法案》附件IV合规映射机制。通过对六位具有丰富经验的软件架构师进行监管覆盖度评估，初步证据表明，RAD-AI将附件IV内容的可覆盖性从约36%提升至93%（平均评分），显著优于现有框架。对两个生产级AI平台（Uber Michelangelo、Netflix Metaflow）的对比分析揭示了标准框架遗漏的八项关键AI特有关切，证明文档缺陷属于结构性问题，而非特定领域局限。此外，一个典型的智慧出行生态系统案例研究进一步暴露了传统符号表示法难以察觉的系统级挑战，包括漂移的级联效应以及差异化合规义务等复杂问题。

title: SAGAI-MID: A Generative AI-Driven Middleware for Dynamic Runtime Interoperability
authors: Oliver Aleksander Larsen, Mahyar T. Moghaddam
abstract: Modern distributed systems integrate heterogeneous services, REST APIs with different schema versions, GraphQL endpoints, and IoT devices with proprietary payloads that suffer from persistent schema mismatches. Traditional static adapters require manual coding for every schema pair and cannot handle novel combinations at runtime. We present SAGAI-MID, a FastAPI-based middleware that uses large language models (LLMs) to dynamically detect and resolve schema mismatches at runtime. The system employs a five-layer pipeline: hybrid detection (structural diff plus LLM semantic analysis), dual resolution strategies (per-request LLM transformation and LLM-generated reusable adapter code), and a three-tier safeguard stack (validation, ensemble voting, rule-based fallback). We frame the architecture through Bass et al.'s interoperability tactics, transforming them from design-time artifacts into runtime capabilities. We evaluate SAGAI-MID on 10 interoperability scenarios spanning REST version migration, IoT-to-analytics bridging, and GraphQL protocol conversion across six LLMs from two providers. The best-performing configuration achieves 0.90 pass@1 accuracy. The CODEGEN strategy consistently outperforms DIRECT (0.83 vs 0.77 mean pass@1), while cost varies by over 30x across models with no proportional accuracy gain; the most accurate model is also the cheapest. We discuss implications for software architects adopting LLMs as runtime architectural components.
title_zh: SAGAI-MID：一种由生成式人工智能驱动的动态运行时互操作性中间件
abstract_zh: 现代分布式系统集成了异构服务、不同版本的REST API、GraphQL端点以及具有专有数据格式的物联网设备，这些组件普遍存在持续性的模式不匹配问题。传统的静态适配器需要为每对数据模式手动编写代码，且无法在运行时处理新的组合。我们提出SAGAI-MID，一种基于FastAPI的中间件，利用大语言模型（LLMs）在运行时动态检测并解决模式不匹配问题。该系统采用五层架构：混合检测（结构差异分析与LLM语义分析相结合）、双重解析策略（按请求进行LLM转换，以及生成可复用的适配器代码），以及三层安全防护机制（验证、集成投票、规则回退）。我们通过Bass等人提出的互操作性策略框架来重构该架构，将原本仅限于设计阶段的抽象转变为运行时能力。我们在10个互操作性场景中评估了SAGAI-MID，涵盖REST版本迁移、物联网到分析系统的桥接，以及跨六家不同提供商的两种大语言模型之间的GraphQL协议转换。表现最佳的配置达到了0.90的pass@1准确率。CODEGEN策略始终优于DIRECT策略（平均pass@1分别为0.83 vs 0.77），而不同模型间的成本差异超过30倍，但准确率并未随之提升；最准确的模型同时也是成本最低的。本文还探讨了软件架构师在将大语言模型作为运行时架构组件时所面临的启示与挑战。

title: Physical Design of UET-RVMCU: A Streamlined Open-Source RISC-V Microcontroller
authors: Abdullah Azhar, Uneeb Kamal, Wajid Ali, Saad Gillani, Dr Suleman Sami Qazi
abstract: This paper presents the design and physical implementation of UET-RVMCU, a lightweight RISC-V microcontroller derived from the UETRV-PCore. Aimed at creating an accessible and flexible open-source RISC-V-based microcontroller, UET-RVMCU simplifies the application-class UETRV-PCore by reducing pipeline stages, removing MMU functionality, and integrating GPIO peripherals. The final GDSII layout was generated using an open-source RTL-to-GDS flow (OpenLane). This project demonstrates the feasibility of transforming an application-class SoC into a feature-rich microcontroller suitable for embedded systems, emphasizing low area, design simplicity, and open-source development.
title_zh: UET-RVMCU的物理设计：一款精简开源的RISC-V微控制器
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了UET-RVMCU这一轻量级RISC-V微控制器的设计与物理实现，该微控制器源自UETRV-PCore。旨在打造一款易于使用且灵活的开源RISC-V微控制器，UET-RVMCU通过减少流水线级数、移除MMU功能并集成GPIO外设，简化了应用级的UETRV-PCore。最终的GDSII版图采用开源RTL到GDS的流程（OpenLane）生成。本项目展示了将一款应用级SoC转化为功能丰富、适用于嵌入式系统的微控制器的可行性，强调了低面积占用、设计简洁性以及开源开发的重要性。

title: Coherent Without Grounding, Grounded Without Success: Observability and Epistemic Failure
authors: Camilo Chacón Sartori
abstract: When an agent can articulate why something works, we typically take this as evidence of genuine understanding. This presupposes that effective action and correct explanation covary, and that coherent explanation reliably signals both. I argue that this assumption fails for contemporary Large Language Models (LLMs). I introduce what I call the Bidirectional Coherence Paradox: competence and grounding not only dissociate but invert across epistemic conditions. In low-observability domains, LLMs often act successfully while misidentifying the mechanisms that produce their success. In high-observability domains, they frequently generate explanations that accurately track observable causal structure yet fail to translate those diagnoses into effective intervention. In both cases, explanatory coherence remains intact, obscuring the underlying dissociation. Drawing on experiments in compiler optimization and hyperparameter tuning, I develop the Epistemic Triangle, a model of how priors, signals, and domain knowledge interact under varying observability. The results suggest that neither behavioral success nor explanatory accuracy alone suffices for attributing understanding. I argue that evaluating artificial epistemic agents requires a tripartite framework -- coherence, grounding, and a proper basing relation linking explanation to action. The systematic separation of knowing-that and knowing-how in LLMs thus challenges assumptions inherited from both epistemology and current AI evaluation practice.
title_zh: 无根基的连贯性，无成功的依附性：可观察性与认识论失败
abstract_zh: 当一个代理能够阐明某事为何有效时，我们通常将其视为真正理解的证据。这一观点预设了有效行动与正确解释之间存在一致性，且连贯的解释能可靠地反映二者。然而，我主张这一假设在当代大型语言模型（LLMs）中并不成立。我提出所谓的“双向一致性悖论”：在不同认识论条件下，能力与根基不仅分离，甚至发生反转。在可观测性较低的领域，LLMs 常常表现出成功的行为，却错误识别导致其成功的机制；而在可观测性较高的领域，它们往往生成准确反映可观察因果结构的解释，却无法将这些诊断转化为有效的干预措施。在这两种情况下，解释的连贯性依然保持完好，从而掩盖了其背后的根本脱节。基于编译器优化和超参数调优中的实验，我构建了一个“认识三角模型”，用以揭示先验、信号与领域知识在不同可观测性条件下的相互作用机制。研究结果表明，仅凭行为成功或解释准确性，都不足以证明理解的存在。我主张，评估人工认知主体需要采用三重框架——连贯性、根基性，以及解释与行动之间恰当的依据关系。LLMs 中“知道什么”与“知道如何做”的系统性分离，挑战了来自传统认识论及当前人工智能评估实践的诸多既有假设。

title: ORACAL: A Robust and Explainable Multimodal Framework for Smart Contract Vulnerability Detection with Causal Graph Enrichment
authors: Tran Duong Minh Dai, Triet Huynh Minh Le, M. Ali Babar, Van-Hau Pham, Phan The Duy
abstract: Although Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have shown promise for smart contract vulnerability detection, they still face significant limitations. Homogeneous graph models fail to capture the interplay between control flow and data dependencies, while heterogeneous graph approaches often lack deep semantic understanding, leaving them susceptible to adversarial attacks. Moreover, most black-box models fail to provide explainable evidence, hindering trust in professional audits. To address these challenges, we propose ORACAL (Observable RAG-enhanced Analysis with CausAL reasoning), a heterogeneous multimodal graph learning framework that integrates Control Flow Graph (CFG), Data Flow Graph (DFG), and Call Graph (CG). ORACAL selectively enriches critical subgraphs with expert-level security context from Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and Large Language Models (LLMs), and employs a causal attention mechanism to disentangle true vulnerability indicators from spurious correlations. For transparency, the framework adopts PGExplainer to generate subgraph-level explanations identifying vulnerability triggering paths. Experiments on large-scale datasets demonstrate that ORACAL achieves state-of-the-art performance, outperforming MANDO-HGT, MTVHunter, GNN-SC, and SCVHunter by up to 39.6 percentage points, with a peak Macro F1 of 91.28% on the primary benchmark. ORACAL maintains strong generalization on out-of-distribution datasets with 91.8% on CGT Weakness and 77.1% on DAppScan. In explainability evaluation, PGExplainer achieves 32.51% Mean Intersection over Union (MIoU) against manually annotated vulnerability triggering paths. Under adversarial attacks, ORACAL limits performance degradation to approximately 2.35% F1 decrease with an Attack Success Rate (ASR) of only 3%, surpassing SCVHunter and MANDO-HGT which exhibit ASRs ranging from 10.91% to 18.73%.
title_zh: ORACAL：一种具有因果图增强的稳健且可解释的多模态智能合约漏洞检测框架
abstract_zh: 尽管图神经网络（GNNs）在智能合约漏洞检测方面展现出巨大潜力，但仍面临诸多显著局限。同质化图模型难以捕捉控制流与数据依赖之间的相互作用，而异构图方法通常缺乏深层次的语义理解能力，因而容易受到对抗性攻击的影响。此外，大多数黑盒模型无法提供可解释的证据，阻碍了专业审计中的信任建立。为应对这些挑战，我们提出ORACAL（可观测的RAG增强分析与因果推理框架），这是一种融合控制流图（CFG）、数据流图（DFG）和调用图（CG）的异构多模态图学习框架。ORACAL通过检索增强生成（RAG）和大语言模型（LLMs）选择性地向关键子图注入专家级安全上下文，并采用因果注意力机制，有效剥离真实漏洞指标与虚假相关性之间的干扰。为提升透明度，该框架引入PGExplainer，生成子图级别的解释，精准识别漏洞触发路径。在大规模数据集上的实验表明，ORACAL达到业界领先性能，相较于MANDO-HGT、MTVHunter、GNN-SC和SCVHunter，最高提升达39.6个百分点，在主基准测试中取得91.28%的峰值宏F1值。在分布外数据集上，ORACAL仍保持强大泛化能力，分别在CGT Weakness和DAppScan数据集上达到91.8%和77.1%的准确率。在可解释性评估中，PGExplainer与人工标注的漏洞触发路径相比，实现了32.51%的平均交并比（MIoU）。面对对抗攻击时，ORACAL将性能下降控制在约2.35% F1降幅以内，攻击成功率（ASR）仅为3%，显著优于SCVHunter和MANDO-HGT（其ASR范围为10.91%至18.73%）。

title: Loop Control Management in Tightly Coupled Processor Arrays (TCPAs)
authors: Dominik Walter, Frank Hannig, Jürgen Teich
abstract: Multidimensional loop kernels often suffer from control overhead that can dominate execution time on parallel loop accelerators. Tightly Coupled Processor Arrays (TCPAs) offload loop control to a global controller (GC), but existing approaches still require hundreds of control signals. We propose a method to derive and aggressively reduce these control conditions from a polyhedral representation of the iteration space, achieving reductions of 15x to 45x in control signals across several benchmarks. We introduce a lightweight GC architecture that evaluates conditions as unions of polyhedra using bounded evaluation units, requiring hardware comparable to a single processing element. Control signals are distributed throughout the array with a minimal number of delay elements resulting in zero-overhead loop control. Our evaluation on PolyBench kernels shows that the entire control flow requires < 10 % of the total array resources.
title_zh: 紧耦合处理器阵列（TCPAs）中的循环控制管理
abstract_zh: 多维循环内核在并行循环加速器上常常受到控制开销的影响，这种开销可能主导执行时间。紧密耦合处理器阵列（TCPA）将循环控制任务交由全局控制器（GC）处理，但现有方法仍需数百条控制信号。我们提出一种方法，通过迭代空间的多面体表示来推导并激进地减少这些控制条件，在多个基准测试中实现了控制信号数量15倍至45倍的缩减。我们引入了一种轻量级的GC架构，利用有限评估单元以多面体的并集形式评估条件，其硬件开销仅相当于一个处理单元。控制信号在阵列中分布时仅需极少量的延迟元件，从而实现零开销的循环控制。我们在PolyBench内核上的评估表明，整个控制流程所占用的阵列资源不足总资源的10%。

title: Debt Behind the AI Boom: A Large-Scale Empirical Study of AI-Generated Code in the Wild
authors: Yue Liu, Ratnadira Widyasari, Yanjie Zhao, Ivana Clairine Irsan, David Lo
abstract: AI coding assistants are now widely used in software development. Software developers increasingly integrate AI-generated code into their codebases to improve productivity. Prior studies have shown that AI-generated code may contain code quality issues under controlled settings. However, we still know little about the real-world impact of AI-generated code on software quality and maintenance after it is introduced into production repositories. In other words, it remains unclear whether such issues are quickly fixed or persist and accumulate over time as technical debt. In this paper, we conduct a large-scale empirical study on the technical debt introduced by AI coding assistants in the wild. To achieve that, we built a dataset of 304,362 verified AI-authored commits from 6,275 GitHub repositories, covering five widely used AI coding assistants. For each commit, we run static analysis before and after the change to precisely attribute which code smells, bugs, and security issues the AI introduced. We then track each introduced issue from the introducing commit to the latest repository revision to study its lifecycle. Our results show that we identified 484,606 distinct issues, and that code smells are by far the most common type, accounting for 89.1% of all issues. We also find that more than 15% of commits from every AI coding assistant introduce at least one issue, although the rates vary across tools. More importantly, 24.2% of tracked AI-introduced issues still survive at the latest revision of the repository. These findings show that AI-generated code can introduce long-term maintenance costs into real software projects and highlight the need for stronger quality assurance in AI-assisted development.
title_zh: AI生成代码背后的债务：一项关于真实世界中AI生成代码的大规模实证研究
abstract_zh: 如今，AI编程助手在软件开发中已被广泛使用。软件开发者越来越多地将AI生成的代码集成到其代码库中，以提升开发效率。以往的研究表明，在受控环境下，AI生成的代码可能存在质量缺陷。然而，我们对AI生成代码在实际生产环境中引入后对软件质量和维护工作的影响仍知之甚少。换句话说，这些缺陷是否会被迅速修复，还是会长期存在并累积为技术债务，目前尚不明确。本文开展了一项大规模的实证研究，探讨AI编程助手在真实世界中引入的技术债务问题。为此，我们构建了一个包含6,275个GitHub仓库中304,362个经验证的AI编写提交的数据库，覆盖了五种广泛使用的AI编程助手。对于每一条提交，我们在变更前后运行静态分析，精确识别出AI引入的代码异味、缺陷和安全问题。随后，我们追踪每个被引入的问题，从其首次出现的提交一直跟踪到仓库最新的版本，以研究其生命周期。研究结果表明，我们共识别出484,606个不同的问题，其中代码异味是最常见的类型，占所有问题的89.1%。我们还发现，几乎所有AI编程助手的提交中，超过15%都会引入至少一个缺陷，尽管不同工具之间的引入率存在差异。更重要的是，有24.2%的被追踪AI引入的问题在仓库的最新版本中仍然存在。这些发现表明，AI生成的代码可能给实际软件项目带来长期的维护成本，凸显了在AI辅助开发过程中加强质量保障的必要性。

title: GEAKG: Generative Executable Algorithm Knowledge Graphs
authors: Camilo Chacón Sartori, José H. García, Andrei Voicu Tomut, Christian Blum
abstract: In the context of algorithms for problem solving, procedural knowledge -- the know-how of algorithm design and operator composition -- remains implicit in code, lost between runs, and must be re-engineered for each new domain. Knowledge graphs (KGs) have proven effective for organizing declarative knowledge, yet current KG paradigms provide limited support for representing procedural knowledge as executable, learnable graph structures. We introduce \textit{Generative Executable Algorithm Knowledge Graphs} (GEAKG), a class of KGs whose nodes store executable operators, whose edges encode learned composition patterns, and whose traversal generates solutions. A GEAKG is \emph{generative} (topology and operators are synthesized by a Large Language Model), \emph{executable} (every node is runnable code), and \emph{transferable} (learned patterns generalize zero-shot across domains). The framework is domain-agnostic at the engine level: the same three-layer architecture and Ant Colony Optimization (ACO)-based learning engine can be instantiated across domains, parameterized by a pluggable ontology (\texttt{RoleSchema}). Two case studies -- sharing no domain-specific framework code -- provide concrete evidence for this framework hypothesis: (1)~Neural Architecture Search across 70 cross-dataset transfer pairs on two tabular benchmarks, and (2)~Combinatorial Optimization, where knowledge learned on the Traveling Salesman Problem transfers zero-shot to scheduling and assignment domains. Taken together, the results support that algorithmic expertise can be explicitly represented, learned, and transferred as executable knowledge graphs.
title_zh: GEAKG：生成式可执行算法知识图谱
abstract_zh: 在问题求解的算法背景下，程序性知识——即算法设计与算子组合的“know-how”——通常隐含于代码之中，在运行之间丢失，且在每个新领域都需要重新工程化。知识图谱（KG）在组织陈述性知识方面已被证明是有效的，但现有的KG范式对表示可执行、可学习的程序性知识为图结构的支持仍然有限。我们提出了**生成式可执行算法知识图谱**（Generative Executable Algorithm Knowledge Graphs, GEAKG），这是一类新型的知识图谱：其节点存储可执行的算子，边编码了学习到的算子组合模式，而图的遍历过程则能生成解决方案。GEAKG具有三个核心特性：**生成性**（拓扑结构和算子由大型语言模型合成）、**可执行性**（每个节点均为可运行的代码）以及**可迁移性**（所学模式可在零样本情况下跨领域泛化）。该框架在引擎层面具有领域无关性：相同的三层架构与基于蚁群优化（ACO）的学习引擎可适用于不同领域，仅通过一个可插拔的本体（\texttt{RoleSchema}）进行参数化配置。两个案例研究——均未使用任何领域特定的框架代码——为该框架假设提供了有力证据：（1）在两个表格基准数据集上，针对70个跨数据集的迁移对进行神经网络架构搜索；（2）组合优化任务中，从旅行商问题（TSP）中学到的知识实现了零样本迁移至调度与分配领域。综合来看，这些结果表明，算法专长可以被显式地表示、学习并作为可执行的知识图谱进行迁移。

title: See it to Place it: Evolving Macro Placements with Vision-Language Models
authors: Ikechukwu Uchendu, Swati Goel, Karly Hou, Ebrahim Songhori, Kuang-Huei Lee, Joe Wenjie Jiang, Vijay Janapa Reddi, Vincent Zhuang
abstract: We propose using Vision-Language Models (VLMs) for macro placement in chip floorplanning, a complex optimization task that has recently shown promising advancements through machine learning methods. Because human designers rely heavily on spatial reasoning to arrange components on the chip canvas, we hypothesize that VLMs with strong visual reasoning abilities can effectively complement existing learning-based approaches. We introduce VeoPlace (Visual Evolutionary Optimization Placement), a novel framework that uses a VLM, without any fine-tuning, to guide the actions of a base placer by constraining them to subregions of the chip canvas. The VLM proposals are iteratively optimized through an evolutionary search strategy with respect to resulting placement quality. On open-source benchmarks, VeoPlace outperforms the best prior learning-based approach on 9 of 10 benchmarks with peak wirelength reductions exceeding 32%. We further demonstrate that VeoPlace generalizes to analytical placers, improving DREAMPlace performance on all 8 evaluated benchmarks with gains up to 4.3%. Our approach opens new possibilities for electronic design automation tools that leverage foundation models to solve complex physical design problems.
title_zh: 看它，放它：基于视觉-语言模型的宏观布局演变
abstract_zh: 我们提出使用视觉-语言模型（VLMs）来解决芯片布局规划中的宏单元放置问题，这是一个复杂的优化任务，近年来已通过机器学习方法展现出令人瞩目的进展。由于人类设计者在芯片画布上排列组件时高度依赖空间推理能力，我们假设具备强大视觉推理能力的VLMs能够有效补充现有的基于学习的方法。为此，我们提出了VeoPlace（视觉进化优化布局）——一种新颖的框架，该框架无需对VLM进行微调，即可利用其生成的建议，将基础布局器的动作约束在芯片画布的特定子区域中。VLM的建议通过一种进化搜索策略，针对最终布局质量进行迭代优化。在开源基准测试中，VeoPlace在10个基准中的9个上超越了现有最佳学习型方法，峰值布线长度减少超过32%。此外，我们还证明了VeoPlace可推广至解析式布局器，在全部8个评估基准上均提升了DREAMPlace的性能，最高提升达4.3%。我们的方法为电子设计自动化工具开辟了新途径，使其能够借助基础模型来解决复杂的物理设计难题。

title: C2RustXW: Program-Structure-Aware C-to-Rust Translation via Program Analysis and LLM
authors: Yanyan Yan, Yang Feng, Jiangshan Liu, Di Liu, Zixi Liu, Hao Teng, Baowen Xu
abstract: The growing adoption of Rust for its memory safety and performance has increased the demand for effective migration of legacy C codebases. However, existing rule-based translators (e.g., \ctorust) often generate verbose, non-idiomatic code that preserves unsafe C semantics, limiting readability, maintainability, and practical adoption. Moreover, manual post-processing of such outputs is labor-intensive and rarely yields high-quality Rust code, posing a significant barrier to large-scale migration. To address these limitations, we present \tool, a program-structure-aware C-to-Rust translation approach that integrates program analysis with Large Language Models (LLMs). \tool extracts the multi-level program structure, including global symbols, function dependencies, and control- and data-flow information, and encodes these as structured textual representations injected into LLM prompts to guide translation and repair. Based on this design, \tool performs dependency-aware translation and adopts a multi-stage repair pipeline that combines rule-based and structure-guided LLM-based techniques to ensure syntactic correctness. For semantic correctness, \tool further integrates execution-based validation with structure-guided reasoning to localize and repair behavioral inconsistencies. Experimental results show that \tool achieves 100\% syntactic correctness on CodeNet and 97.78\% on GitHub, while significantly reducing code size (up to 43.70\%) and unsafe usage (to 5.75\%). At the project level, \tool achieves perfect syntactic correctness and an average semantic correctness of 78.87\%, demonstrating its effectiveness for practical and scalable C-to-Rust migration.
title_zh: C2RustXW：通过程序分析与大语言模型实现的面向程序结构的C到Rust翻译
abstract_zh:
随着Rust在内存安全和性能方面的日益普及，对遗留C代码库进行有效迁移的需求也不断增长。然而，现有的基于规则的翻译工具（如\ctorust）通常生成冗长且不符合Rust惯用风格的代码，同时保留了不安全的C语义，导致代码可读性差、难以维护，限制了其实际应用。此外，对这些输出进行手动后期处理工作量大，且极少能产出高质量的Rust代码，成为大规模迁移的重大障碍。

为解决上述问题，我们提出\tool——一种面向程序结构的C到Rust翻译方法，该方法将程序分析与大型语言模型（LLMs）相结合。\tool能够提取多层级的程序结构信息，包括全局符号、函数依赖关系以及控制流与数据流信息，并将这些信息编码为结构化文本表示，注入LLM提示中，以指导翻译过程并实现代码修复。基于这一设计，\tool采用依赖感知的翻译策略，并引入多阶段修复流水线，结合基于规则的方法与结构引导的LLM技术，确保生成代码的语法正确性。

为进一步保障语义正确性，\tool还集成了基于执行的验证机制与结构引导的推理能力，用于定位并修复行为上的不一致。实验结果表明，\tool在CodeNet上实现了100%的语法正确率，在GitHub数据集上达到97.78%；同时显著减少了代码体积（最高降低43.70%）和不安全用法（降至5.75%）。在项目级别，\tool实现了完全的语法正确性，并达到了平均78.87%的语义正确率，充分证明了其在实际、可扩展的C到Rust迁移中的有效性。

title: Design and Implementation of an IoT System and Logical Prototype Model Using the Circuitverse Open-Source Tool
authors: Meenakshi Kashyap, Pinky Yadav, Malarvizhi A, Sanjula S, Sonal Kumar, Kaustubh Kumar Shukla
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Intelligent Sustainable Systems (ICISS)
abstract: The key intention of this work is to create an innovative path to implement IoT based device and its logical prototype. An open-source tool named as CircuitVerse is used to achieve the targeted goal. Several models have been designed logically tested and validated. The foremost benefit of this unique work is one can be able to implement a complicated IoT based architecture into a simple logical prototype model. Since, the entire process and costing is very low comparatively so it is more useful for the students and researchers because of its accuracy specially. In this research work through this paper the prototype model is correctly simulated and logically verified as well as shown the output by making use of LEDs in CircuitVerse environment. It has been also noted that the modular & hierarchical modelling method accomplished the system understandable, reusable, and better at finding and fixing bugs. Additional advantage of this work is mapping of prototype based logical design with the real hardware. So, in many ways it has been found that CircuitVerse tool is much effective for the IoT system design and implementation since, before hardware implementation it is providing one path to test and have some idea. This work has a huge impact in education for modelling, testing, and validating intelligent connected systems. The markable point is even in coming future people may think on the integration of the real-time cloud stages, the growth of physical hardware prototypes, which has unlimited applications like smart cities, healthcare and several monitoring cum industrial automation. Thus, it's a good multidisciplinary work in that people from different background can get involved and contribute for a better outcome.
title_zh: 基于Circuitverse开源工具的物联网系统设计与逻辑原型模型实现
abstract_zh: 本研究的核心目标是探索一种创新的方法，用于实现基于物联网（IoT）的设备及其逻辑原型。为此，采用了一款开源工具——CircuitVerse，成功达成了既定目标。研究中设计并逻辑测试、验证了多个模型。这项工作的最大优势在于，能够将复杂的物联网架构简化为一个易于理解的逻辑原型模型。由于整个过程成本极低，因此对广大学生和研究人员具有极高的实用价值，尤其在准确性方面表现突出。本文通过CircuitVerse环境对原型模型进行了正确仿真，并进行了逻辑验证，同时利用LED灯直观展示了系统输出结果。此外，研究还发现，采用模块化与分层建模方法，使系统结构更清晰、可复用性更强，且在故障排查与修复方面效率更高。另一显著优势是，该工作实现了基于原型的逻辑设计与实际硬件之间的映射。由此可见，CircuitVerse工具在物联网系统的设计与实现中表现出高度有效性——它为硬件实现前提供了一个可靠的测试路径，使开发者能提前获得系统运行的直观认知。本研究在教育领域具有深远影响，特别是在智能互联系统的建模、测试与验证方面。尤为值得一提的是，未来人们有望将实时云平台与物理硬件原型进一步融合，从而拓展出无限的应用前景，如智慧城市、智慧医疗以及各类监测与工业自动化系统。因此，这是一项极具跨学科意义的工作，不同背景的研究者均可参与其中，共同推动成果的优化与提升。

title: Token Energy Optimization (Semantic Cost Proxy) in GPT-2 Using Counterfactual Semantic Budgets: MetabolicGPT-2
authors: Abhijit Nayak, Raj Bhowmik
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Information and Communication (ICAIIC)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in settings where generation quality must be balanced against strict resource or verbosity constraints. Existing approaches to constrained text generation typically operate at decoding time, or optimize coarse proxies such as output length, with limited visibility into the semantic importance of individual tokens. In this work, we present MetabolicGPT-2, a GPT-2-based language model that learns an explicit token-level semantic cost proxy and generates under user-specified semantic budgets. Each token is assigned a non-negative cost that combines a corpus-driven synthetic prior with a counterfactual semantic cost (CSC) computed from a sentence encoder by measuring embedding change when a token is removed. We cast budget control as constrained optimization: minimize language modeling loss subject to an expected total proxy-cost constraint. We implement this by augmenting GPT-2 with a cost head, learnable token-cost parameters, a Lagrangian dual variable, and an online affine calibration layer that aligns proxy costs to CSC at the sequence level. Experiments on WikiText-2 illustrate that learned costs correlate with semantic importance and that budget settings shift the model toward lower-cost continuations while largely preserving fluency. Throughout, the term “energy” refers to this semantic proxy cost rather than direct hardware joules.
title_zh: 基于反事实语义预算的GPT-2中令牌能量优化（语义成本代理）：代谢GPT-2
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在越来越多的应用场景中被部署，这些场景要求在生成质量与严格的资源或冗长度约束之间取得平衡。现有的受限文本生成方法通常仅在解码阶段进行控制，或通过优化输出长度等粗略代理指标，对单个词元的语义重要性缺乏充分的洞察。在本研究中，我们提出 MetabolicGPT-2，这是一种基于 GPT-2 的语言模型，能够学习一个显式的词元级语义成本代理，并在用户指定的语义预算下进行生成。每个词元都被赋予一个非负的成本值，该值结合了基于语料库的合成先验与从句向量编码器计算出的反事实语义成本（CSC），后者通过衡量移除某个词元后嵌入表示的变化来评估。我们将预算控制建模为约束优化问题：在满足预期总代理成本约束的前提下，最小化语言建模损失。为此，我们在 GPT-2 的基础上引入了成本头、可学习的词元成本参数、拉格朗日对偶变量，以及一个在线仿射校准层，用于在序列层面将代理成本与 CSC 对齐。在 WikiText-2 数据集上的实验表明，所学得的成本与词元的语义重要性高度相关，且在不同预算设置下，模型会倾向于生成更低成本的延续内容，同时基本保持流畅性。在整个研究中，“能量”一词指的是这种语义代理成本，而非直接的硬件焦耳能耗。

title: Agentic AI in DevOps: Boosting Software Automation and Collaboration
authors: Mohammed Akour, Mamdouh Alenezi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Security and Governance (ICAISG)
abstract: This paper explores the transformative impact Agentic Artificial Intelligence (AI) can bring into DevOps, mainly in automation, collaboration, and decision-making. We combine controlled experiments and real deployment in a fintech setting to show that the integration of Agentic AI into CI/CD pipelines drastically reduces deployment times, detects errors better, and encourages multi-disciplinary collaboration. Our results show a 36% reduction in test cycle time and a 65% decrease in rollback frequency. Deployment assurance was rated a high 4.6/5 from developers when using the AI assistant-giving developers' confidence in this assistant. This paper offers practical guidelines and architectural blueprints for organizations intending to implement Agentic AI, followed by discussions on factors of ethical, operational, and technical concern for building explainable and trustworthy AI systems.
title_zh: 智能代理AI在DevOps中的应用：提升软件自动化与协作效率
abstract_zh: 本文探讨了代理型人工智能（Agentic AI）在DevOps领域带来的变革性影响，主要体现在自动化、协作与决策制定方面。通过结合受控实验与金融科技环境中的实际部署，我们证明将代理型AI集成到CI/CD流水线中，可显著缩短部署时间、更有效地检测错误，并促进跨学科协作。研究结果显示，测试周期时间减少了36%，回滚频率下降了65%。当使用AI助手时，开发人员对部署保障的评分高达4.6/5，反映出开发者对该助手的高度信任。本文为有意实施代理型AI的组织提供了实用指南与架构蓝图，并进一步讨论了构建可解释且值得信赖的人工智能系统所涉及的伦理、运营和技术层面的关键考量因素。

title: Explainable Artificial Intelligence in Software Engineering: Current Trends, Gaps, and Future Directions
authors: Adam Khan, Asad Ali, Muhammad Ismail Mohmand, Mahdi Zareei, Rajesh Roshan Biswal
abstract: Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) has gained increasing attention for improving the transparency of artificial intelligence systems. However, there is a less clear understanding of its adoption and research patterns in the field of Software Engineering (SE).This systematic literature review focuses on the use of XAI within SE and the prospects of cross-domain learning. To achieve this, twenty-nine studies applying XAI in Software Engineering (SE), published between March 2020 and April 2024, were reviewed, and their findings were compared with XAI practices reported in healthcare and finance. The findings show that SE research mostly uses local explanation techniques: 86% of the studies employ instance-level methods, such as Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME), whereas global explanation techniques that provide an overall view of the model are used less frequently. In terms of application domains, XAI studies in SE are mainly focused on software defect prediction, while other domains, such as software testing, and effort estimation remain comparatively underexplored. Regarding implementation tools, scikit-learn is the most frequently used machine learning (ML) library in XAI-based SE studies, and less often, TensorFlow, PyTorch, and R based-frameworks are used. The current study is more comprehensive in terms of scope compared to the previous review, as it addresses four research questions, examines the types of explanations, and clearly mentions the utilization of ML libraries. The findings indicate that future SE studies should incorporate global explanation techniques, diversify XAI methods, and extend the research to underrepresented SE tasks. In addition, clear reporting of tools and libraries is essential to enhance reproducibility. Overall, although XAI in SE is progressing, the domain remains at an early stage, and insights from other fields may help improve interpretability, reliability, and trust in SE systems.
title_zh: 可解释人工智能在软件工程中的应用：当前趋势、研究空白与未来方向
abstract_zh:
可解释人工智能（XAI）在提升人工智能系统透明度方面获得了越来越多的关注。然而，目前对XAI在软件工程（SE）领域中的采纳情况及研究模式的理解仍不够清晰。本系统性文献综述聚焦于XAI在软件工程中的应用以及跨领域学习的前景。为此，我们回顾了2020年3月至2024年4月间发表的29项将XAI应用于软件工程的研究，并将其发现与医疗和金融领域中报道的XAI实践进行了对比。

研究结果表明，当前软件工程领域的XAI研究主要采用局部解释技术：86%的研究使用实例级方法，如局部可解释模型无关解释（LIME），而能够提供模型整体视图的全局解释技术则较少被采用。在应用领域方面，XAI在软件工程中的研究主要集中于软件缺陷预测，而其他领域如软件测试和工作量估算等仍相对未被充分探索。在实现工具方面，scikit-learn是基于XAI的软件工程研究中最常用的机器学习（ML）库；此外，TensorFlow、PyTorch和R等框架也偶有使用。

相较于以往的综述，本研究在范围上更为全面，不仅提出了四个研究问题，还分析了不同类型的解释方法，并明确指出了所使用的机器学习库。研究结果表明，未来软件工程领域的研究应更多引入全局解释技术，丰富XAI方法的应用多样性，并将研究拓展至当前代表性不足的软件工程任务中。同时，清晰地报告所用工具和库对于提高研究的可复现性至关重要。

总体而言，尽管XAI在软件工程领域正逐步发展，但该领域仍处于早期阶段。借鉴其他领域的经验，有望进一步提升软件工程系统中的可解释性、可靠性与可信度。

title: Agentic AI for Recon, Threat Analysis and Mitigation Reporting
authors: Kalaivani T, Charanya P, Abhindhira KP, Barath Kumar L, Santhosraj BS, Srinithi S
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
abstract: Digital attacks in the ever-changing digital environment are becoming highly complex, more common, and dynamic, and this has become a major threat to organization of all sizes. In this paper, the researcher introduces the design of an autonomous and intelligent cybersecurity system that will carry out reconnaissance, threat reporting, and mitigation of websites and IP addresses. Based on an agentic AI architecture, the system uses dedicated AI agents to perform tasks through information collection, vulnerability scanning, and threat pattern matching and result interpreting, which do not require the training of new models. Instead, it runs on the top of existing Large Language Models (LLM), such as GPT and open-source ones, to comprehend and argue about complex security situations A User Interface is used to enter missions, an Orchestrator is used to sequence tasks, Agent modules are used to interact with industry-standard pentesting tools (such as Nmap, Nikto, and SQLmap), a Safety module precludes actions that are beyond its purview, and a Reporter module provides actionable insights. Its framework is focused on automation, adaptability, and scalability, and also limits the use of manual analysis and allows quicker and more precise threat detection and remedies. By means of continuous learning and effective management of various penetration testing situations, this approach becomes a viable way of enhancing cybersecurity measures in small organizations and learning institutions.
title_zh: 用于侦察、威胁分析与缓解报告的代理型人工智能
abstract_zh: 在不断变化的数字环境中，网络攻击正变得日益复杂、频繁且动态化，已成为各类组织面临的主要威胁。本文中，研究者介绍了一种自主化与智能化的网络安全系统设计，该系统能够对网站和IP地址进行侦察、威胁报告及缓解处理。基于代理型人工智能（AI）架构，该系统利用专用AI代理通过信息收集、漏洞扫描、威胁模式匹配以及结果解读来完成任务，无需训练新的模型。相反，它运行于现有的大型语言模型（LLM）之上，如GPT及开源模型，以理解并分析复杂的安全状况。用户通过用户界面输入任务指令，编排器负责任务顺序调度，代理模块则与行业标准渗透测试工具（如Nmap、Nikto和SQLmap）交互，安全模块可防止超出其权限范围的操作，而报告模块则提供可执行的安全洞察。该系统的框架注重自动化、适应性与可扩展性，最大限度减少人工分析的依赖，实现更快、更精准的威胁检测与应对。通过持续学习和对多种渗透测试场景的有效管理，该方法为小型组织和教育机构提升网络安全防护能力提供了切实可行的解决方案。

title: VOAPI2: a Vulnerability-Oriented Testing Framework for RESTful APIs
authors: Lübin Xu, Zhuolin Zeng, Zhen Tang, Biao Xiao, Dong Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Communication Networks and Machine Learning (CNML)
abstract: With the rapid proliferation of Web services, RESTful APIs have become a fundamental building block of modern software systems. As APIs increasingly expose critical business logic and sensitive data, ensuring their security has emerged as a pressing challenge. However, most existing RESTful API testing tools primarily emphasize functional correctness and robustness, and thus often fail to effectively uncover security vulnerabilities that do not manifest as obvious crashes or server errors.In this paper, we present VOAPI2, a vulnerability-oriented inspection framework for RESTful APIs that explicitly targets security flaws by leveraging the intrinsic relationship between API functionality and vulnerability types [1]. Unlike conventional API fuzzers that explore endpoints indiscriminately, VOAPI2 adopts a guided testing strategy that first identifies security-critical endpoints by analyzing semantic cues in API specifications.
